text.title: llamaindex-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for llamaindex-basics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Re-ingesting the same directory repeatedly via SimpleDirectoryReader without incremental sync or dedup logic, creating duplicate nodes in the index.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: SimpleDirectoryReader has no incremental mtime tracking; re-running ingestion appends new nodes without deduplicating modified files. Either (a) clear the index and re-ingest, (b) implement a version hash check before re-indexing, or (c) use LlamaCloud for managed incremental updates.

failure-mode.description: Building a large in-memory VectorStoreIndex and expecting it to stay resident across server restarts without explicit persistence.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: In-memory indices are garbage-collected on restart. Always persist to a vector store (local SQLite + embeddings or remote Qdrant) and reload on startup.

failure-mode.description: Never inspecting `response.source_nodes` to verify retrieval quality, blindly trusting node scores.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Vector similarity scores are relative, not absolute. A node with score 0.9 might still be off-topic if the query is ambiguous. Always inspect source nodes and verify that the synthesizer received good context.
